特記事項: 【合併】総合日本学習科目（政治Ｂ）（CIEC）/Government and Politics in Japan B（国際学部）
授業方法: This course will be conducted face to face in a regular classroom setting from the second class, while the first class will be held online.

第1回:
Introduction
【Startup Week】 "Online class（Simultaneous two-way format）"／ 《90minutes》
  URL will be uploaded in this syllabus.
Assigned reading to be completed before coming to class.  Approximately 2 hour study.  Students' understanding will be checked in class.
第2回:
Peace and Democracy in Postwar Japan (1)
 【Core Weeks①】"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》

Assigned reading to be completed before coming to class.  Approximately 1-hour study.  Students' understanding will be checked in class.  Start preparing for a research proposal.  Feedback will be given in class.

 【Core Weeks ⑪】"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Research and presentation preparation will be required. Approximately 15 to 20-hour study between Sessions 2 and 11. Feedback will be given after the presentation in class.
第14回:

【Flex Hours】"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》

トピック.授業計画1:
1. Weekly assigned reading
2. Preparation for one oral report
3. Preparation for research presentation
トピック.授業外学習2: Please complete assigned readings before coming to class each time.  In addition,  reading and watching daily news on Japan will be required in order to familiarize yourselves with currernt Japanese government, politics and foreign policy agendas.

教科書/Required texts1: The Crisis of Liberal Internationalism: Japan and the World Order
参考書/Reference books1: A Modern History of Japan: From Tokugawa Times to the Present, 4th Edition
参考書/Reference books2: Critical Issues in Contemporary Japan, Second Edition
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic:
NA

各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。
DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。
各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。

備考Note0: ＊若干回休講、補講を行う可能性があります。補講を行う場合は、学部指定の補講日、もしくは講師と受講生の都合のよい日時に、受講生と相談の上、決定し実施します。There may be some class cancellations and make-up classes subsequently.  Make-up classes would be held either on days designated by the school or on mutually covenient days/time periods, which would be decided upon consultations between the lecturer and students.
